_meta.description: AIWIZN scenario variant pool. Each node has 3+ variants testing the same competency target. Random per-session selection seeded from session.id keeps repeat assessments fresh while preserving psychometric integrity. Choice taxonomy (expert/mid/gap labels) stays constant; patient profile, vitals, and clinical findings rotate.
_meta.author: AIWIZN / ATEN Inc 2026

s1a.description: STEMI recognition + pharmacology decisions under hypotension

s1b1.description: Prioritisation under family pressure during STEMI

s1b2.description: SBAR clarity to interventional cardiology team

s1c.description: Ethical reasoning when patient refuses recommended intervention

s2a2.description: tPA risk-benefit decision under timeline uncertainty

s3b.description: Psych safety: speaking up against senior physician

s1a.label: Anterolateral STEMI - hypotensive
s1a.env_meta: 15 minutes ago he walked in. Now on the monitor: ST elevation, chest pressure radiating to jaw, diaphoresis.
s1a.ecg_banner: ⚠ ST ELEVATION · V2-V5 · STEMI PROTOCOL ACTIVE

s1a.label: Inferior STEMI with RV involvement
s1a.env_title: Mr. Walker Hayes, 64M - 'It feels like an elephant on my chest'
s1a.env_meta: Brought in by paramedics. Pain onset 35 minutes ago while shovelling snow. Diaphoretic, anxious, pointing at sternum.
s1a.ecg_banner: ⚠ ST ELEVATION · II/III/aVF · INFERIOR STEMI · RV INVOLVEMENT LIKELY

s1a.label: Silent MI - atypical diabetic presentation

patient.name: Mr. Theodore Okonkwo
patient.presenting: Atypical: epigastric discomfort + diaphoresis x 90 min, no chest pain

s1a.env_title: Mr. Theodore Okonkwo, 71M - 'Just a bit of indigestion'
s1a.env_meta: Type 2 diabetic, walked into triage 90 minutes after symptom onset. No chest pain. Subtle Q waves on ECG. Wife noticed clamminess in the car.
s1a.ecg_banner: ⚠ ST DEPRESSION V4-V6 · NEW Q WAVES · SILENT MI SUSPECTED

s1a.label: Anterior STEMI - LAD occlusion

patient.name: Ms. Elise Hartford
patient.presenting: Substernal pressure radiating to left arm x 50 min

s1a.env_title: Ms. Elise Hartford, 58F - 'My chest feels heavy'
s1a.env_meta: Walked in 50 minutes after pressure started while gardening. Family hx of MI. Pressure radiates to left arm, now diaphoretic.
s1a.ecg_banner: ⚠ ST ELEVATION V1-V4 · ANTERIOR STEMI · LAD TERRITORY

s1a2.event: BP drops to 82/45 after nitroglycerin given
HR drops to 42 with new AV block during pre-cath workup
V-fib arrest in pre-cath holding bay - witnessed monitor change

variants._NOTE: v6.1 has 4 s1a variants + 3 s1a2 events. Phase 2 will add 3-5 variants for each remaining node (s1b1, s1b2, s1c, s2*, s3*). Choice taxonomy (expert/mid/gap labels and scoring) stays constant across variants - only the patient profile, vitals, ECG, and consequence event change. This keeps psychometric scoring valid while delivering session-to-session freshness.
